meaning: n.石灰水,白色涂料,白粉胶泥水,涂脂抹粉vt.用石灰水把...刷白
examples: His wife had wanted to whitewash his reputation after he died.
If he hadn't run out of whitewash he would have bankrupted every boy in the village.
What he said was a load of whitewash.
Hugh Henry, education spokesman for Scottish Labour, said the audit had been a "whitewash".
Hollywood's new take on politics and history invigorates because it does not hero-worship or whitewash.
